microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's resilience validation program into a **platformized system**, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual drills and drive $14M+ impact across enterprise partners including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining self-service scheduling requirements that reduced operational toil by 30% and shifted execution to a scalable, driver-style self-service model.
Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects across the fleet.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a **sovereign-cloud isolation test** tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract requiring airtight stakeholder alignment.
Built an internal AI agent to automate drill planning workflows, cutting **planning cycle time by 39%** and increasing drill capacity by 21% without adding headcount.

Defined success metrics and OKRs for the resilience program, translating ambiguous executive goals into structured team-level goals and measurable outcomes that informed quarterly roadmap prioritization.
Communicated roadmap priorities and experiment results to audiences ranging from engineering leads to C-suite, building alignment across partner orgs on shared resilience goals and launch sequencing.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows, running user demos and training that drove utilization across 14 key teams and saved 37 engineering hours monthly.
Shaped the product roadmap by conducting **11+ discovery interviews** with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing the addition of intent-based YAML generation.
Improved data retrieval by implementing metadata standards and migrating docs to an AI-powered **semantic search tool**, cutting lookup time by 83% for internal engineering users.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and cut region launch time by 28% by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams.
Drove customer discovery with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual toil** per region launch, then built a product roadmap to automate critical paths and reduce friction.
Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to target automation gaps and prioritize high-impact investments with data-backed confidence.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the OS migration strategy and mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations on a two-sided operations platform.
Prioritized a backlog of **40+ high-priority tickets** across sprint cycles, facilitating Agile ceremonies that stabilized the developer intake process and unblocked cross-functional delivery.
Drove alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement automated CI/CD pipelines, **accelerating software deployment cycles by 25%** and reducing friction for end users.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, reducing friction and improving the customer experience through a new CRM workflow.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversion rate by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google Business profile for local search.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify margin leaks and surface data-driven recommendations for pricing model updates.

skills_priority: Data Analysis & Success Metrics (SQL, Power BI, Python)
tailoring_notes: 'Reducing friction' from the JD is woven into microsoft_ft bullet#2 (self-service model), amazon_robotics bullet#3 (deployment friction), and pro_painters bullet#1 (invoicing lifecycle friction).
'Two-sided marketplace' framing appears in amazon_robotics bullet#1 ('two-sided operations platform') mirroring Lyft's driver-rider marketplace language from the JD.

'Customer discovery' and 'user empathy' from the JD surface explicitly in microsoft_2022 bullet#2 ('drove customer discovery with 20+ service teams') and microsoft_2023 bullet#2 ('11+ discovery interviews').

'Success metrics' and 'data analysis' from the JD experience section are mirrored in microsoft_ft bullet#6 ('defined success metrics and OKRs...measurable outcomes that informed roadmap prioritization').
'Communicate roadmaps, priorities, experiments' from the JD responsibilities maps directly to microsoft_ft bullet#7, using near-identical vocabulary to signal fluency with Lyft's PM operating model.
